sanskrit: अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम्। यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः॥
transliteration: aho bata mahat pāpaṃ kartuṃ vyavasitā vayam / yad rājya-sukha-lobhena hantuṃ sva-janam udyatāḥ
literal_meaning: Alas, how strange it is that we are determined to commit great sins, driven by greed for royal happiness, ready to kill our own people.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences a sudden moral awakening, recognizing that the pursuit of power and pleasure has seduced him into dehumanizing his relatives, reducing them to obstacles. This verse captures the moment when the ego’s narrative collapses and the psyche confronts the dissonance between its values and its imminent actions, revealing how desire can override the superego’s prohibitions.
psychological_state: A state of acute moral vertigo where one sees the abyss between who they believed they were and what they are about to do for gain.
trigger_scenario: A mid-level manager realizes they are about to sabotage a colleague’s promotion to secure their own bonus, suddenly feeling nauseated by how ambition has eclipsed their self-respect.
anchor_text: Greed for power can blind us to the humanity of those we love.
